title: Exercises: The Grover Oracle
description: Practice building various types of oracles

exercises.title: Multi-Solution Oracle
exercises.description: Build an oracle that marks all even numbers in a 3-qubit system.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def even_oracle(n_qubits):
    """Mark all even numbers."""
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
oracle = even_oracle(3)
print("Diagonal:", np.diag(oracle))
print("Even numbers marked: ", end="")
for i in range(8):
    if oracle[i,i] == -1:
        print(i, end=" ")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def even_oracle(n_qubits):
    """Mark all even numbers."""
    N = 2**n_qubits
    oracle = np.eye(N)
    for x in range(N):
        if x % 2 == 0:
            oracle[x, x] = -1
    return oracle

oracle = even_oracle(3)
print("Diagonal:", np.diag(oracle))
print("Even numbers marked:", [i for i in range(8) if oracle[i,i] == -1])

exercises.title: SAT Oracle
exercises.description: Build an oracle for: (x0 OR x1) AND (x1 OR x2) AND (NOT x0 OR NOT x2)
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def sat_oracle(n_qubits):
    """Oracle for the given SAT formula."""
    # Your code here
    pass

oracle = sat_oracle(3)
print("Satisfying assignments:")
for x in range(8):
    if oracle[x,x] == -1:
        bits = f"{x:03b}"
        print(f"  {x} = {bits}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def sat_oracle(n_qubits):
    """Oracle for: (x0 OR x1) AND (x1 OR x2) AND (NOT x0 OR NOT x2)"""
    N = 2**n_qubits
    oracle = np.eye(N)
    
    for x in range(N):
        x0 = (x >> 0) & 1
        x1 = (x >> 1) & 1
        x2 = (x >> 2) & 1
        
        clause1 = x0 or x1
        clause2 = x1 or x2
        clause3 = (not x0) or (not x2)
        
        if clause1 and clause2 and clause3:
            oracle[x, x] = -1
    
    return oracle

oracle = sat_oracle(3)
print("Satisfying assignments:")
for x in range(8):
    if oracle[x,x] == -1:
        bits = f"{x:03b}"
        print(f"  {x} = {bits} (x2={bits[0]}, x1={bits[1]}, x0={bits[2]})")
